basics.name: SHASHWATH V R
basics.summary: Aspiring Software Engineer with strong foundations in Data Structures, Algorithms, and Object-Oriented Design. Proven experience in building scalable backend systems and full-stack applications. Skilled in distributed systems, concurrency, containerization, and cloud-native architectures.

experience.title: Software Developer Intern – Rampex Technologies (Aug 2025 – Feb 2026)
bullets: Implemented backend APIs and processing workflows in a cloud-native LMS platform, improving availability and reducing deployment failures by 30%.
Collaborated on debugging production issues, optimizing database queries, and automating deployment workflows, improving overall system performance and maintainability.

projects.name: LANturn – Network-Wide Power Orchestration System
projects.description: Built a centralized orchestration system managing 300+ machines, ensuring reliable remote execution. Designed retry mechanisms and failure-handling logic to improve reliability of Wake-on-LAN operations. Implemented scheduling strategies and backend optimizations, improving operational efficiency by 40%. Simulated failure scenarios to validate recovery workflows and reduce manual intervention by 80%.

projects.name: Terminus Assess – Linux-Based Assessment Infrastructure Platform
projects.description: Designed and stress-tested a Linux-based assessment system handling 100+ concurrent users, focusing on availability and fault isolation. Built secure Docker-based execution environments ensuring reproducibility and system stability. Implemented automated evaluation pipelines and monitoring checks, reducing operational overhead by 50%. Optimized database indexing and query execution plans, improving system throughput by 35%.

projects.name: FinSense – Data Processing & Analytics Engine
projects.description: Real-time streaming pipelines to continuously analyze live market data, news, transactions, and customer behavior. Engineered optimized data-processing pipelines, reducing execution time by 45% and memory usage by 30%. Designed modular components enabling reproducible processing and easier fault isolation.

leadership: Winner – Code Cubicle Hackathon (GeekRoom × Microsoft × Pathway)
